title: NightVision: Warm
description: A NightVision spin-off that adds a reddish-orange tint to the screen for optimal warmth for your eyes
body:
### **Be Sure To Follow On Instagram to get future updates and to resolve quires:** **[@nv.labs.official](https://www.instagram.com/nv.labs.official/)**

### 💡 About this pack
This pack is a warm variant of the original [NightVision: Standard](https://modrinth.com/resourcepack/nv_1.21.x) project, keeping the same lightmap-based visibility boost while adding a subtle orange tint that makes nighttime skys feel cozier without washing out contrast or depth. It works in survival, creative, and on servers that allow client-side resource packs, and stays lightweight since it only edits Minecraft’s core lightmap instead of relying on external shader loaders.

_**[Note: This pack doesn't change the way caves look at night]**_

### **🎮 Use cases**

- Mining and caving without placing torches everywhere.
- Building at night while still clearly seeing block shapes and colors.
- Exploring, raid prep, and general gameplay when you want better visibility but not flat                    fullbright.

### **⚙️ Compatibility**
Made for Minecraft 1.21.x (also works with 26.1). Works with vanilla and most vanilla-style packs if this pack is above them in the resource pack list. May conflict with packs or shaders that modify the same lightmap.fsh (fullbright, night-vision fixes, etc.). Also works with [Sodium](https://modrinth.com/mod/sodium) when [PolyTone](https://modrinth.com/mod/polytone) is installed.

### **Be sure to check out our other project:** [DarkGUI](https://modrinth.com/resourcepack/dark_gui), [SmartComms](https://modrinth.com/mod/smartcomms),  [NightVision: Standard](https://modrinth.com/resourcepack/nv_1.21.x)
